title: Separating Hyperplane Theorem for Convex Sets
description: A visual explanation of how a hyperplane can separate two disjoint convex sets, a fundamental concept in convex optimization.
relationships: Sets A and B are disjoint convex sets
Hyperplane separates the two sets such that all points in A are on one side and all points in B are on the other
Normal vector w defines the orientation of the hyperplane
Offset b determines the position of the hyperplane
